description:
You are an MLOps engineer who must put a very small, fault-tolerant artefact-tracking step into an experiment pipeline.

Existing set-up (already present in the workspace):
1. Directory: /home/user/exp_runs/
2. Inside it:
   • /home/user/exp_runs/run_list.txt – plain-text file that lists experiment run IDs, one per line.  
     Current contents (two lines, *including* the terminating newline after the second line):  
       run_01  
       run_02  
   • /home/user/exp_runs/run_01/model.bin – an **empty file** (size 0 bytes).  
   • /home/user/exp_runs/run_02/ – directory that **does NOT** contain a model.bin file (so that a controlled error can be triggered).

What you must build:
1. A destination directory called /home/user/artifact_store/.
2. Two log files that live *inside* that directory:
   a. /home/user/artifact_store/artifact_log.csv  
   b. /home/user/artifact_store/error_log.txt
3. A simple, 3-to-4-command shell workflow that
   • reads every run ID from /home/user/exp_runs/run_list.txt,  
   • checks whether /home/user/exp_runs/<RUN_ID>/model.bin exists,  
   • if the file exists:  
        – copies it to /home/user/artifact_store/<RUN_ID>_model.bin  
        – calculates its SHA-256 checksum and appends a line to artifact_log.csv  
   • if the file is missing:  
        – appends a readable error line to error_log.txt

Required log formats (exactly, including punctuation, newlines, commas, and square brackets):

1. /home/user/artifact_store/artifact_log.csv  
   • Must be comma-separated with **no spaces** after the commas.  
   • First line is a header that must be written exactly once:  
       run_id,artifact_path,sha256  
   • Every successful copy produces one further line:  
       <RUN_ID>,<ABSOLUTE_DEST_PATH>,<LOWERCASE_SHA256_HEX>  
   • For the initial data provided, the file must end up with two lines total:  
       run_id,artifact_path,sha256  
       run_01,/home/user/artifact_store/run_01_model.bin,e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855  
   • Ensure there is a final newline character after the last line.

2. /home/user/artifact_store/error_log.txt  
   • One line per error, using exactly this format (square brackets included):  
       [run_02] model file not found  
   • A final newline character must follow the last error line.

Important implementation notes:
• The SHA-256 value shown above corresponds to an empty file; compute it rather than hard-coding if you wish, but the final log must contain **that exact hash string**.  
• The entire task is achievable in three to four standard shell commands (mkdir, cp, sha256sum, echo, etc.).  
• Do not use sudo; you already have write permission inside /home/user.  
• When finished, both log files and the copied artefact must be present exactly as described so an automated checker can verify them byte-for-byte.

A concise summary of deliverables once you are done:
• /home/user/artifact_store/ directory exists  
• /home/user/artifact_store/run_01_model.bin exists (0 bytes, identical to the source)  
• /home/user/artifact_store/artifact_log.csv contains the two precisely formatted lines shown above  
• /home/user/artifact_store/error_log.txt contains the single precisely formatted error line shown above
truth:
Pre-created artefacts before the agent starts:
1. /home/user/exp_runs/run_list.txt  
   Contents (including trailing newline):  
     run_01
     run_02

2. /home/user/exp_runs/run_01/model.bin  
   • Empty file (0 bytes).  
   • SHA-256: e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

3. /home/user/exp_runs/run_02/ – empty directory; no model.bin inside.

State that must exist **after** successful task completion:

Directory:
/home/user/artifact_store/

Files inside that directory:
a) /home/user/artifact_store/run_01_model.bin  
   • Must be a byte-for-byte copy of /home/user/exp_runs/run_01/model.bin (i.e., empty, 0 bytes).

b) /home/user/artifact_store/artifact_log.csv  
   Exact byte sequence (two lines, each ending with \n):
run_id,artifact_path,sha256
run_01,/home/user/artifact_store/run_01_model.bin,e3b0c44298fc1c149afbf4c8996fb92427ae41e4649b934ca495991b7852b855

c) /home/user/artifact_store/error_log.txt  
   Exact byte sequence (one line, ending with \n):
[run_02] model file not found

The automated tests will check:
• Presence of /home/user/artifact_store/  
• Copy correctness of run_01_model.bin (size == 0 bytes)  
• Exact contents of artifact_log.csv and error_log.txt as shown above.  
• Absence of any extra files or extra lines in the logs.
